thesis: Fast, story-shaped Shorts that turn surprising history, human performance, and everyday systems into memorable explanations.

contentLanes.description: Unexpected historical explanations with a reversal or contrast. The hook should set up what people assume, then flip it.
exampleHooks: Why Roman concrete outlived modern buildings
Why marathon distance is such a weird number
The accidental invention that saved millions of lives
Why Napoleon wasn't actually short
The war that lasted 335 years with zero casualties
Why we shake hands — and what it replaced

contentLanes.description: What a benchmark means for the body in real life. Ground abstract performance numbers in physical, visceral detail.
exampleHooks: How fast the fastest humans actually move
What running a marathon does to your body
Why you can't hold your breath for 5 minutes — but this guy can
How much force a punch from a heavyweight actually carries
What happens to your muscles after 24 hours without sleep
The ceiling of human reaction time — and why it exists

contentLanes.description: Simple explanations of invisible systems and mechanisms that people interact with daily but never think about.
exampleHooks: What turbulence actually is
How GPS knows where you are
Why your phone battery dies faster in the cold
How traffic jams start from literally nothing
Why elevator doors wait exactly that long
How tap water gets from a dam to your glass

contentLanes.description: How money, pricing, and economic systems actually work behind the scenes. No financial advice — just mechanism explanations.
exampleHooks: Why prices end in .99 — and why it still works
How credit scores are actually calculated
Why movie popcorn costs more than the ticket
What happens to your money the second you tap your card
Why airline tickets change price every time you check
How insurance companies decide what you pay

contentLanes.description: Objects, interfaces, and environments that are deliberately designed to influence your behaviour — often without you noticing.
exampleHooks: Why supermarkets put bread at the back
The reason stop signs are octagons
Why casino carpets are so ugly — on purpose
How Instagram's pull-to-refresh was stolen from a slot machine
Why hospital walls are that specific colour
The font on road signs was engineered to save lives

contentLanes.description: Comparisons that make incomprehensible numbers or sizes suddenly click. Always anchor to something the viewer already understands.
exampleHooks: If the Sun were a basketball, Earth would be this far away
How much data the internet moves every single second
The actual size of a blue whale's heart
How many humans have ever lived vs how many are alive now
If you stacked every shipping container moved last year
How deep the ocean actually goes — floor by floor

contentLanes.description: The origin stories behind rules, laws, and conventions that seem arbitrary but exist for a specific reason.
exampleHooks: Why we drive on the left in some countries and right in others
Why the drinking age is 18 in most countries but 21 in the US
The actual reason your pilot dims the cabin lights before landing
Why chefs wear those tall white hats
Why manhole covers are round — it's not what you think
The real reason QWERTY exists

contentLanes.description: The chemistry and biology behind cooking, taste, and food production. Make the science feel like a plot twist.
exampleHooks: Why onions make you cry — and how to stop it
What actually happens when you toast bread
Why mint feels cold even though it's not
How your tongue decides what tastes good
Why microwaved food heats so unevenly
The reason honey never expires — literally never

contentLanes.description: How modern technology actually works at the layer most people never see. Demystify without dumbing down.
exampleHooks: How your WiFi router decides who gets bandwidth
What actually happens when you Google something
How noise-cancelling headphones know which sounds to kill
Why your face unlocks your phone but a photo doesn't
How autocorrect predicts your next word
What happens inside a chip when you press a key

contentLanes.description: Biological systems and natural phenomena explained as if they were engineering solutions — because they basically are.
exampleHooks: How ants solve traffic better than humans
Why birds fly in a V — the physics is wild
How trees communicate underground
Why your eyes can see more shades of green than any other colour
How salmon navigate thousands of kilometres back to the exact stream they were born in
The engineering behind a spider web

contentLanes.description: Cognitive biases, psychological effects, and brain mechanics explained with a 'so that's why I do that' payoff.
exampleHooks: Why you always think of the perfect comeback too late
The reason you can't tickle yourself
Why songs get stuck in your head — and how to get them out
How your brain decides what to remember and what to forget
Why time feels slower when you're bored
The trick your brain uses to fill in your blind spot

contentLanes.description: Statistics, metrics, and numbers that are commonly cited but wildly misleading once you understand what's behind them.
exampleHooks: Why 'average salary' means almost nothing
The problem with BMI — and why doctors still use it
Why crime stats can say opposite things depending on who's presenting them
How a 90% effective vaccine doesn't mean what you think it means
Why your phone's battery percentage is a rough guess
The misleading stat behind 'you eat 8 spiders a year in your sleep'

contentLanes.description: AskReddit-style comment compilations on gameplay footage. Picks a viral post and reads the top comments while a Reddit-styled card fills in word-by-word.
